front: Painting: red-haired woman in white dress floating in a boat on the river to Camelot, inspired by a Tennyson poem
back: The Lady of Shalott (John William Waterhouse)

front: Painting: woman weaves a tapestry in front of a mirror reflecting Camelot; title quotes 'I Am Half-Sick of Shadows'
back: The Lady of Shalott / I Am Half-Sick of Shadows (John William Waterhouse)

front: Painting: Shakespeare character sitting on a tree branch over a lilypad-covered river (not the Millais version with Elizabeth Siddal)
Artist: painted Ophelia five times; one version is also titled 'Gather Flowers While Ye May'
Painting: water nymphs abduct a companion of Hercules; removed from and returned to the Manchester Art Gallery in 2018
back: Hylas and the Nymphs (John William Waterhouse)

front: Painting: mythological hunter admires his reflection in a pool while a partly bare-chested red-headed woman looks on; at the Walker Art Gallery, Liverpool
Artist: Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood member known for literary and mythological subjects; described as 'non-Millais Pre-Raphaelite'
Painting: women in pink and blue leaning over (title is a Herrick poem about gathering rosebuds)
back: Gather Ye Rosebuds While Ye May (John William Waterhouse)

front: Painting: maroon-clad woman wrapping a tan scarf around a man she is about to kiss, based on a Keats poem
back: La Belle Dame sans Merci (John William Waterhouse)

front: Painting: patron saint of music sleeping in a chair as kneeling angels play violin and bowed lute for her
